entry.title: 3rd SAP Stammtisch in Cyprus: 100 Years of Experience and 5 Key Takeaways
entry.content:
What a great session! Last week, the third SAP Stammtisch here in Cyprus brought together an amazing group of people once again. Great atmosphere, open exchange, and above all, concentrated expert knowledge. We did a quick calculation and realized we had about 100 years of collective SAP experience at the table.

Our topic of the evening was S/4HANA Transformation. We asked ourselves: what are the absolute essentials to make this project a success? The result of our discussion is these 5 central takeaways:

1. Collaboration and Communication S/4HANA is not just an IT project. Silos must be broken down. Business and IT need to speak a common language; otherwise, development will miss the actual requirements.

2. Commitment and Clear Decisions Agile is good, but a lack of commitment is fatal. You need a strong mandate and decisions that stick so the project can gain real momentum.

3. Process Design, Documentation, and Simplification No pure "lift and shift"! The transformation is a chance to cut off old habits. Moving "Back to Standard" only works through radical simplification of processes.

4. Change Management and Enablement The best technology is useless if the users don't get on board. Change must be managed professionally through training, communication, and real empowerment of key users.

5. Master Data Quality and Data Cleansing The classic issue that is often addressed too late. "Garbage in, garbage out" applies here more than ever. Without massive data cleansing beforehand, the go-live will be rocky.

A huge thank you to all participants — Damla Toraman, Feyzi Göktas, Eugen Lust, Igor Perelmann, Raphael Klosek, Sabine Clemens — for this inspiring evening! It proves once again that nothing replaces personal exchange.

Which of these points do you see as the biggest stumbling block?

#SAP #S4HANA #SAPCommunity #Cyprus #Transformation #ERP #Networking

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP - APIM - Domain-Centric Routing Pattern (DCRP): Governing APIs via CPI Provider - Part I
entry.content:
@ATU 

Thank you very much for comment.

The main trade-off is between centralized governance (security, compliance, observability) and domain team autonomy for rapid changes. DCRP solves this with clear separation — centralized APIM manages the contract (routing rules, policies — changes quarterly), while domain teams deploy iFlows and update KVM autonomously (changes weekly). Adding a new vendor = 30 seconds (1 KVM line), zero proxy changes. In practice, organizations vary: Banking/Pharma favor strong governance (max security, moderate speed); Startups prefer high autonomy (max speed, controlled risk); Enterprises adopt hybrid (centralized APIM + decentralized CPI = balance between governance and agility).

This pattern originated from deep study of the standard Dynamic Routing Pattern in API Management and is the result of a proof-of-concept implementation. In Part III (currently in progress), I'll provide the full framework for PoC implementation in 30 minutes — no modifications to policies or KVMs required, just testing. Scaling this pattern depends entirely on discipline, organization, and governance.

Stu Ztu: "governance should enable agile delivery within clear policies, not delay it".

The Commander | Viana.

entry.title: When flagging the pricing condition for deletion a second condition also marked for deltion.
entry.content:
Hello everyone, We have an issue when flagging a condition record for deletion. When deleting a condition which has the infinite date as validity end, another condition record with the same price is also marked for deletion.

In the above screenshot, we are deleting the condition valid from 01.05.2028 to 31.12.9999 and system also flags the condition 01.01.2026 to 01.31.2026 for deletion. Both these condition has the same amount as price. The condition type is a copy of PR00 condition type. 

The only connection i can think off is, originally the condition ending on 31.12.9999 had the starting date as 01.01.2026 and it was moved to 01.05.2026 as the other conditions were added. 

Appreciate your help

SKR

entry.title: system Picks Only One Freight Order Type When Planning Different FU Types based on condition det.
entry.content:
We are using capacity doc. creation with the condition /SCMTMS/TOR_TYPE in the planning profile to determine the Freight Order (FO) type based on Freight Unit (FU) attributes (e.g., FU Type, DG, Origin Country).

Observed behavior in manual and automatic assign/ assign selected items in cockpit:

  • When planning a single FU the FO type determination works correctly.
  • When planning multiple FUs with different FU types, the system combines them into one FO and applies one FO type (typically from the first FU).
    ➜ This is wrong for our process; each FU type should lead to its own FO type.

Tried already

  • Verified /SCMTMS/TOR_TYPE condition in planning profile.
  • Tested with VSR and manual planning.
  • Maintained FU–FU incompatibility (split by FU Type / DG).
    → System shows a warning, but still allows saving one FO (no auto-split, no separate FO types).

Requirement

  • When selecting multiple FUs with different FU types, the system must automatically create separate FOs, each with the correct FO type according to /SCMTMS/TOR_TYPE.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Steps by Step Guide to enable Joule in Fiori launchpad for S4HANA 2025 Cloud Private Edition
entry.content:
Hi 
Great blog. However, a few things seem to be missing.
We had Joule running on S/4HANA 2023 PCE (still in PoC phase), but after the upgrade to 2025, Joule stopped working across all environments (DEV, TST, and PRD)

During our analysis, we discovered a new parameter that appears to be undocumented yet.

DAS_WEBCLIENT_PLUGIN


This parameter is Activated by default and contains following URL https://q2c-joule-pilot-multi-system.eu12.sapdas.cloud.sap/resources/public/webclient/s4/

The thing is, that isn't our subaccount; it seems to be a demo account. (Maybe it's a version of Joule that allows connecting more than one system? The name suggests so.)

After the upgrade, this parameter now covers the Technical Catalog settings by default

And as a result the following error occurs 

Notes 3652218 - "Refused to frame" error message when using Joule in FLP  and 3673511 - Troubleshooting for Joule page display issues when opening Joule in SAP products do not resolve the issue because we don't have access to this subaccount.
Official Joule documentation (https://help.sap.com/doc/de3af3c0f81642dbaa4d36172ed57a72/CLOUD/en-US/79bfc83ab386450c8cd9c7937ce26a3a.pdf) doesn't mention about.

And there are two possible solutions for this.

1. Update DAS_WEBCLIENT_PLUGIN with our Joule URL

2. Remove DAS_WEBCLIENT_PLUGIN parameter.

I have raised a ticket to OSS for this. 
Artur

entry.title: Re: Timesheet completeness validation
entry.content:
Dear @vasco_keizer,

1. Standard SAP option for Project Managers (pre-billing control)

Notify Missing Times (F2556) is the standard way for Project Management to check whether team members time is up to date missing time before starting billing proposal / billing preparation and to follow up with consultants

More details below:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2556')/S36

https://learning.sap.com/courses/managing-projects-and-resources-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services/managing-time-recording-and-approvals

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003525043

2. Standard automation (reduce manual chasing)

If you want a systematic everyone must be complete by X day mechanism, SAP provides a standard scheduled job template to send automated emails for missing time

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0bebd08dffca45afa67b1f751199afd0/f3be0d588d9ab244e10000000a4450e5.html

There is also a standard monitoring app commonly used to track reminder notifications, Monitor Missing Time Notifications (F3031)

More details below:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/change-viewing-rights-for-fiori-app-quot-monitor-missing-time-notifications/qaq-p/12414729

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F3031')/S36

3. When you need a global completeness dashboard

If your requirement is an enterprise-level view like recorded hours + leave vs planned working time work schedule/availability per employee/week then a reporting/dashboard layer is often the best approach in Public Cloud

SAP-supported building blocks for reporting:

  • Timesheet reporting CDS references: I_TimeSheet and I_TimeSheetRecord

SAP Note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003495527

  • Availability/target-hours side and related checks: I_TimeOverviewByWrkAgrmtAndDte and API guidance API_MANAGE_WF_AVAILABILITY

SAP Note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003654922

  • IMPORTANT: Missing time can be false positive/negative if target hours/availability isn’t maintained correctly

SAP Note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003391722

Summary:

  • Operational PM gate before billing use Notify Missing Times (F2556) per project/team
  • Automation schedule the Missing Time Entries email notification job weekly or multiple times per week
  • Enterprise transparency (optional) build an Embedded Analytics dashboard using released CDS objects to show missing hours by consultant/week and optionally drill down by project

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Automated Exporting and downloading of report to local shared folder
entry.content:
Hi everyone! 👋

I'm working on automating the download of certain reports from SAP to a shared network folder on my PC, and I've hit a small roadblock. Hoping someone here has dealt with this before!

What we've done so far:

  • ✅ Created a shared folder with read and write access
  • ✅ Had SAP whitelist the shared folder IP
  • ✅ Developed a custom Z-program and tested it
  • ✅ Getting "downloading successfully" message

The issue: Despite the success message, no files are actually appearing in the shared folder 

What we found: After some research, it seems we need to:

  • Mount the IP address to the application server
  • Configure the path as a directory in AL11

My question: Has anyone faced a similar situation? If so, could you please walk me through the exact steps needed to get this working? We're running S/4HANA Private Cloud.

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated! Thanks in advance! 🙏

entry.title: Re: Turning on "Lexicographical" setting for Product masters
entry.content: This restriction is by design in S/4HANA Cloud. Once numeric material numbers exist in a tenant, SAP does not allow the lexicographical indicator to be switched on, even if those materials are archived or inactive. Unfortunately, SAP will not enable this setting manually in Production either, as SSCUI 102320 is treated as a system-critical configuration and must be consistent across tenants. Since you haven’t started activities in PROD yet, the safest path is to enable lexicographical numbering before any material creation, or consider a tenant refresh once the legacy constraints are cleared. I’d strongly recommend raising an incident with SAP to confirm options, but based on current behavior, waiting or refreshing is usually the only supported route.

entry.title: Re: How do you manipulate the values of STXH, COBL, VBPA during Sales order creation or VA01?
entry.content: For VA01 you normally don’t change those tables directly. Use SD user exits or BAdIs instead. For sales order data and partners (VBPA), check the exits in MV45AFZZ. For accounting-related fields (COBL / STXH), the values are set later, so a BAdI like SD_ACCOUNTING is usually the right place. Updating the tables directly isn’t supported.

entry.title: The Fortress of Modern Commerce: Why RISE with SAP is the Answer to the "Digital Siege"
entry.content:
Fortress of Modern Commerce: Why RISE with SAP is the Answer to the "Digital Siege"

As a leader for SAP Enterprise Cloud Services, I spend my days talking to executives who share a common, growing anxiety: "Are we truly protected?"

In recent months, the global manufacturing landscape has been shaken by the events at Jaguar Land Rover. It wasn't just a data leak; it was a full-scale "digital siege" that halted production for weeks. While many focus on the "how," the industry knows the "why": a critical failure in patch management. This is a stark reminder: in a hyper-connected world, an unpatched system is an open door.

The Stakes: Half of the World’s GDP

When we talk about RISE with SAP, we aren't just talking about "moving to the cloud." We are talking about the engine of the global economy.

Did you know? Approximately 77% of the world’s transaction revenue touches an SAP system. On any given day, RISE with SAP facilitates transactions equivalent to half of the global GDP.

If SAP stops, the world stops. This is why our security posture isn't just "industry standard"—it is mission-critical infrastructure.

The Patch Management Advantage: Closing the Window of Vulnerability

The JLR incident highlighted a universal truth: hackers don't always "break in"; they "log in" through vulnerabilities that should have been closed months prior.

In a traditional on-premise or unmanaged setup, the responsibility for patching lies with your internal teams, who are often stretched thin. With RISE with SAP Private Cloud, we have never been breached precisely because we eliminate that "window of vulnerability."

We don't just host; we automate the hygiene. From the OS to the database, SAP manages the lifecycle of updates so that a "known vulnerability" never becomes your "business outage."

In the Room: A Conversation with a Concerned CIO

Here is how I discuss this proactive defense with our clients:

Client: "We saw the JLR fallout. It seems their down-time was a result of delayed patches. How does RISE prevent that same human error or delay in our organization?"

Me: "That’s the core of the value proposition. In your current environment, patching is a project you have to schedule. With RISE, it’s a service we deliver. We take the 'human' delay out of the equation by maintaining a rigorous, automated patch cycle. We handle the technical debt so you don't have to worry about a patch being missed because a team was too busy with a rollout."

Client: "But can we really trust a third party with that level of control?"

Me: "Think of it this way: We manage 50% of the world's GDP. Our survival depends on our ability to keep those systems secure. To this day, the SAP Private Cloud perimeter remains undefeated because our dedicated Security Operations Centers (SOC) treat a single missing patch as a critical threat. We close the doors before the hackers even reach the gate."

Resilience as a Competitive Advantage

Digital resilience is the new gold standard. Moving to RISE with SAP is an insurance policy for your brand's reputation. By standardizing your core and handing the management of security updates to the experts, you move from a reactive posture to a proactive one.

Next Steps

The question is no longer if an attack will happen, but how fast your defenses can adapt. Are you still manually managing your security gates, or are you in a managed cloud designed to withstand the storm?

entry.title: Project Resource Demand - Service Activity Type Actualization & Revaluation of Projects
entry.content:
Hello dear SAP gurus, 

   Please forgive me if I'm missing something terribly simple, my client is going to use Cloud Public 2602 and I'm trying to discover the newly introduced functionalities and would like to clear one thing. In EPPM the design expectation is using the project structures in profile projects with revenues.

   I've created a project and a WBS, an internal resource demand for the WBS, assigned resource to a worker ( WBS, worker assigned to the same cost center), maintained worker's service level plan cost rate and spend some time to the project via My Timesheet application successfully. 

   I can monitor the costs by (allocated time * plan cost rate) in WBS, I also configured one of the rule line of the predefined splitting structure for the specific activity on service level as well. Actual cost rate calculation has been done and I could see the acual cost rate in "Manage Cost Rates - Actual" application, the output is consistent.

   Before all, I got to mention that I've changed the activity type main data as follows : Default Variance Values for Actual Allocation ->  Actual Price Indicator -> From none to 5 "Actual price, automatically based on activity". I know how material ledger handles the variances and revaluate the activity consumption but here I made an assumption that there might be some sort of a layer similar to ML which allows same functionality. Still, I went to actual costing run and wanted to observe if the activity is selected but the specific service activity is not selected as supposed.

   The question is, is it possible to handle the service activity p/a variances as a period end process and allocate them to the WBS in Cloud Public 2602 ? My clients expectation is monitoring the plan activities through the period and allocate the variances after calculated from "service" cost centers. In my point of view, there is no such layer or function to fullfill this expectation in standard however sort of work arounds can be applied such as manual distribution of variances to the projects by Universal Alloc. with rec. factor of these activities period end quantities.

   Thanks in advance for your interest and guidance.

entry.title: Re: Learning expiration email notification setup
entry.content:
Hi

It is not possible to setup a stagerred number of days at the global APM level to trigger Expiration and Overdue emails. It is driven off the initial and the threshold days.

You may look at customising this at the item level if required - but that will still follow the initial and the threshold day format.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Succefactors learning integrate with KnowBe4
entry.content:
Hi @likahee

Just a key considerations before you look further into the integration.

- Some of the non-standard OCN providers (in this case KnowBe4) don't sync completions instantly as they are batch-driven. SF expects a near real time completion for a seamless update so it is important you carefully consider where the completions are tracked (i.e., which system becomes your source of truth for compliance).

Thank you

entry.title: ALTER SYSTEM ADD STATEMENT HINTにおける「FOR <sql_statement>」の指定方法について
entry.content:
現在、現行環境と比較して新環境の処理時間が遅く、業務に支障が生じており、
新環境を本番稼働できない可能性があるという大きな課題があります。
 
このため、処理時間が遅いSQL文に対してHINT句を指定する方法について調査を行っています。
 
SAP Note DIGITS の「6. 文のヒントはどのように管理できますか。」にて、
以下のSQLコマンドの説明があることを確認しました。
ALTER SYSTEM ADD STATEMENT HINT ( <hints> ) [OVERRIDE] [COMMENT '<comment>'] FOR <sql_statement>
 
本コマンドについて、以下の２点をご教示いただけますでしょうか。
 
１．FOR <sql_statement> の <sql_statement> には、STATEMENT HASH ではなく、SQL文そのものを指定することは可能でしょうか。
 
２．（上記 1 において SQL文を指定可能な場合）
　　<sql_statement> に、以下の例のように % などのワイルドカードを指定することは可能でしょうか。
　　目的としては、1回のコマンドで複数のSQL文に対してHINT句を指定できるかを確認したいと考えています。
 
　　ALTER SYSTEM ADD STATEMENT HINT ( USE_HEX_PLAN ) FOR 'SELECT % FROM "Z_CDS_VIEW_A" WHERE %'
 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: ALTER SYSTEM ADD STATEMENT HINTにおける「FOR <sql_statemen...
entry.content:
＞１．FOR <sql_statement> の <sql_statement> には、STATEMENT HASH ではなく、SQL文そのものを指定することは可能でしょうか。
▼
ご認識通りになります。
 
＞２．<sql_statement> に、以下の例のように % などのワイルドカードを指定することは可能でしょうか。
▼
不可になります。
<sql_statement> を対象SQL文の全文で指定する必要があります。

・参考資料
2142945 - FAQ: SAP HANA Hints
2400006 - FAQ: SAP HANA Statement Hints

entry.title: Integration Suite issue with Deep Entity create: dependent entities missing
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am facing a rather interesting issue that I have never seen before. I have the following scenario: An integration flow in Integration Suite that connects to an OData service on-premise, with cloud connector setup (the Odata service is hosted on premise, ECC.

 

All is well but for one flow: I have an scenario that i will forward a payload like this one:

<?xml version='1.0' encoding='UTF-8'?><TransportEntitySet><TransportEntity><CodigoRota>15573914</CodigoRota><DataHoraImportacaoRota>2026-01-30T11:28:00</DataHoraImportacaoRota><DataHoraInicioRota>2026-01-30T16:22:00</DataHoraInicioRota><DataHoraFimRota>2026-01-30T16:23:00</DataHoraFimRota><NomeRota>0060012623</NomeRota><Status>2</Status><IdDeposito/><Entregas><Entrega><CodigoEntrega>118350098</CodigoEntrega><DataHoraChegada>2026-01-30T16:22:00</DataHoraChegada><DataHoraFimAtendimento>2026-01-30T16:23:00</DataHoraFimAtendimento><Pedidos><Pedido><IdPedido>000352640</IdPedido><NumeroPedido>000352640</NumeroPedido><Remessa>0310794518</Remessa><ComentarioPedido>Lorem Ipsum</ComentarioPedido><CodigosBarra/></Pedido></Pedidos></Entrega></Entregas><Entregas><Entrega><CodigoEntrega>118350099</CodigoEntrega><DataHoraChegada>2026-01-30T16:23:00</DataHoraChegada><DataHoraFimAtendimento>2026-01-30T16:23:00</DataHoraFimAtendimento><Pedidos><Pedido><IdPedido>000352641</IdPedido><NumeroPedido>000352641</NumeroPedido><Remessa>0310794520</Remessa><ComentarioPedido>Texto 01 / Texto 02 / Texto 03 / Texto 04</ComentarioPedido><CodigosBarra/></Pedido></Pedidos><Pedidos><Pedido><IdPedido>000352642</IdPedido><NumeroPedido>000352642</NumeroPedido><Remessa>0310794521</Remessa><ComentarioPedido>Texto 01 / Texto 02 / Texto 03 / Texto 04</ComentarioPedido><CodigosBarra/></Pedido></Pedidos></Entrega></Entregas></TransportEntity></TransportEntitySet>

As the OData adapter only accepts XML for POST methods, we'll send the payload as above as the result of the conversion of a JSON to XML, so looking at the XML seems correct. The definition of the service is as below (from the associations panel):

 

 

However when the OData adapter is called, the message is transformed to the following payload, where each of the composite (expanded) entities Entregas and Pedidos have only one entry, namely the last one.

I Thought it was a cardinality problem, but it doesn't seem like that. I'll try to assign referential constraints based on the id of the message but it should not have any.

Has anyone faced something similar? i think i'll reshape the service and maybe add some referential constraints

appreciate any inputs

Erick

entry.title: SAP MM PO MESSAGE VE 108
entry.content: Good day to everyone! I've got some problem with a tax determination in MM. Condition type - MWST. I created a personal condition table (501) with only field - Tax code. Also I changed access sequence for MWST with that table. I entered condition records for a new table via MEK31. Problem! When I create purchase order (ME21N) I enter a tax code on the "Invoice" tab. But, tax in the calculation schema doesn't determined. In pricing analysis there is an error message VE 108. After updating prices (G - Copy pricing elements unchanged and redetermine taxes) - tax succesfully determines. Can I customize updating process automatically? Where? In schema? Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP-CPI-Package-Domain-Centric-Pattern (PDCP):Solving Package Sprawl at Scale-PART II
entry.content:
Thanks for the detailed write-up. I have some concerns about this approach from both practical implementation and presentation perspectives:

1. Language & Cultural Sensitivity The heavy use of military metaphors throughout ("Commander," "battlefield," "blast radius control," "surgical precision," "operational theater") is problematic. Given the current global situation with ongoing conflicts affecting millions of people, this language feels tone-deaf and inappropriate for technical documentation. Integration architecture is not warfare—it's collaborative problem-solving. Professional technical content should use neutral, inclusive language that resonates globally across diverse audiences. Terms like "mitigation strategy," "impact isolation," or "failure containment" convey the same technical concepts without the militaristic framing.

2. Complexity vs. Value Introducing SAP API Management as a default proxy layer in front of SAP CI adds significant complexity, cost, and maintenance overhead. For many organizations, direct SAP CI endpoints with proper security are sufficient. The DCRP+PDCP synergy may be over-engineered for most use cases.

3. Package Grouping Philosophy The domain-centric consolidation (39 packages → 4) addresses a real problem, but the solution may be too rigid. Traditional grouping by third-party system or specific business process often works better in SAP CI's architecture, which differs fundamentally from SAP PO. One Salesforce package, one Shopify package, etc., with clear naming conventions, provides good isolation and maintainability without forcing everything into four buckets.

4. Technical User Management The concern about credential management (39 users → 12) is overstated. Modern credential management tools (CyberArk, HashiCorp Vault, Azure Key Vault) handle this well, and separate users per package provide better security isolation, clearer audit trails, and proper separation of concerns. The "credential nightmare" scenario assumes poor tooling and manual processes that shouldn't exist in 2026.

5. Self-Proclamation Declaring oneself a "SAP BTP Integration Suite Expert" and "The Commander" undermines credibility. Let the technical content demonstrate expertise rather than relying on self-assigned titles.

Core Value The naming convention framework (iFlow DNA) and focus on reducing package sprawl through standardization are valuable contributions. However, the architectural pattern may be significantly more complex than necessary for most organizations.

A simpler, proven approach:

  • Enforce clear naming standards across all iFlows
  • Group packages logically by third-party system or bounded business process
  • Use modern credential management tools with automated rotation
  • Add API Management only when there's a clear business case (external partner access, rate limiting, monetization)
  • Avoid premature optimization—start simple, add complexity only when justified

Would be interested in hearing from others who've implemented this pattern at scale and whether the operational benefits justified the implementation complexity.

entry.title: Re: Installation Success Factor xxxxxxx not visible via c...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

If you are not able to create case for the new SF installation, then very likely your S-user is not the cloud admin for that SF installation and have no authorization 'Display Cloud Data' assigned on that SF installation number.

Could you pls kindly check via the My Important Contact page and find the current active cloud admin for that SF installation number?

Then pls kindly contact the corresponding SF cloud admin to get the authorization 'Display Cloud Data' assigned on that SF installation number.

Check KBA 2598170 - What is the Display Cloud Data authorization? to know more about this authorization.

In case you have issue to find the correct SF installation or you still cannot create SF case after getting the required authorization, feel free to create a case using component XX-SER-FORME.

Thanks and regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Guidance on best adapter choice for inbound integration to SAP Cloud Private via CPI
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am working on an inbound integration scenario using SAP Cloud Integration.
An external (non-SAP) application will send data through CPI to SAP Cloud Private, mainly for creating and updating business objects.

From a design perspective, I am currently leaning toward OData services because they are standardized, REST-based, support CRUD operations, and are easier for future consumers such as web or mobile applications.

However, I would like to validate this assumption against real project experience and community best practices.

I would appreciate your advice on the following:

  1. In similar inbound scenarios, which adapter is most commonly recommended?

    • OData

    • IDoc

    • RFC/BAPI

    • SOAP

    • other approaches?

  2. Under what conditions would you recommend not using OData, even if the requirement is mainly CRUD?

  3. Looking at performance, monitoring, extensibility, and long-term maintenance, which option tends to be the most sustainable?

  4. If the backend is built using modern paradigms such as RAP/CDS, does that naturally position OData as the primary interface?

I am open to alternative recommendations if other approaches are considered more suitable.

Thank you very much for sharing your experience.

entry.title: Re: Sap BTP innovation
entry.content:
Hey Atul, BTW, great questions.
Let me attempt to answer for you:

1. Are teams using BTP as a strategic platform, or has it become just another integration and extension toolbox?
BTP is designed to be more than an integration or extension toolbox. It’s meant to unify data, analytics, AI, application development, and automation across SAP and non-SAP landscapes. 
If you have S/4 HANA as your EPR, then what would BTP add?
Decoupled Innovation, meaning, build new features and apps outside S/4HANA, and avoid breaking the ERP. Keeping the #CoreClean
You tap BTP for powerful analytics, predictive trends, and AI-driven insights that goes beyond S/4HANA’s built-in tools.
SAP is evolving in a crazy pace, and BTP helps you keep up the pace.

2. Which BTP capability delivers the most real value today: Integration Suite, CAP, Event Mesh, or AI services?
Each of these have their own unique usecases and purposes to serve. 
Integration Suite: Rapid, reliable connections across SAP and non-SAP apps, which iscore for hybrid landscapes.
CAP: Flexible app development, low-code tools to build custom extensions without ERP risk.
Event Mesh: Enables real-time, event-driven business processes, and is great for reactive, scalable solutions.
AI: Although still in a very early stage, but promises to automate decisions, unlocks insights from data, and add intelligence to SAP workflow.

3. How are you preventing BTP from becoming the new custom-code dumping ground?
This I feel more of a organization's "best pratices" problem. Preventing BTP from turning into a dumping ground isn’t just about technology, or SAP, it’s about disciplined engineering, strong governance, and shared accountability across teams.
While SAP is well-rounded and provides numerous solutions OOTB, it is flexible so we could limitlessly build, but if left ungoverned, then the "custom-code" plague will spread like a wildfire.
Always advisable to use best practices, design guidelines, conduct a feasibility test first thing first before designing a solution, do proper code reviews, and 4 eye check, enforce naming conventions, documentation rules. Again, all this is to be regulated/ enforced/ implemented by the architects of a firm.

entry.title: Re: Inquiries regarding SAP FOR ME site login
entry.content:
Hello customer, 

For several S-users linked with the same email address, to change the default login S-user: 
1. Access SAP ID Service.
2. Click 'Log Out'.
3. Log in again using the desired S-user ID. 
4. After successfully changing, the updated S-user will appear first on the subsequent page.

Pls also reset S-user individual password per KBA 1808560, and refer to KBA 3656247 - Issue with automatic S-user selection during login - SAP Universal ID (UID) for more detailed information and background.

Thanks and regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: International Editable SALV Day 2026 – Year 18
entry.content:
SALV is 18 years already, i had no idea.
I still remember writing my first SALV a bit over 10 years ago with my first steps into SAP development.
Once I got to know the framework everything fell together nicely and list reports could be developer quickly, given it took somewhat longer for the editable version to get all the actions implemented.

Later on I had to adapt the classic ALV. Oh boy, was that a different cup of tea having to deal with so much more details in the development process :D.

Nowadays it is all CDS list reports and RAP applications in case it is not read only. 
Things sure have changed. Nonetheless, in the meantime I think I might be quicker putting together a simple RAP than an SALV simply because it is done so much more.
Personally I have grown fond of the CDS views combined with RAP to create full Fiori applications by means of Fiori Elements manipulation via annotations. Of course it all depends on what your internal SAP strategy is and what kind of system you are working on as in my eyes RAP really started popping of with S/4 2023.

thanks for the interesting read 🙂

entry.title: Re: Project Resource Demand - Service Activity Type Actua...
entry.content:
Good day @S0025758586 

Thank you for your question.

Your assessment is in line with the public cloud 2602. In public cloud, you do not have a standard period end layer equivalent to Material Ledger for service activity consumption on WBS which settles activity price variances to WBS automatically in the same way ML revalues materials.

From a standard point of view, what you can do is:

  • Recalculate actual activity on cost centers, See the following references for more details:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c56f622a2edf491b9f1b596b55587009/ae106483aa5a4eb89e3cb47a941fb533.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c56f622a2edf491b9f1b596b55587009/99f93cbadd0e48779791419e87c25b8f.html

  • View and manage rates in the rate apps, See

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c56f622a2edf491b9f1b596b55587009/9fca5b12d8df4591a240100305da6357.html for more details.

  • Carry out actual price revaluation via dedicated project valuation process not via ML style service layer behaviour,
  • For controlled redistribution when you need period end allocation logic, you will have to use Universal Allocation which is the standard framework.

Question: Is it possible to handle the service activity p/a variances as a period end process and allocate them to the WBS in Cloud Public 2602?

Answer: No, not with an automatic service Layer like ML. I suggest you use the standard project revaluation and or Universal Allocation based period end design.

In summary (for your client):

  • Your client’s expectation is common, however, the technical model in Public Cloud is different from ML material revaluation.
  • The design supported in Public Cloud is rate calculation plus revaluation/allocation process, not an automatic ML style service variance engine.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: In Joule Skill workflows, is there any way to create a custom function to process input paramete
entry.content: In Joule Skills, input parameters are handled as strings, so if you need structured data like a list, you’ll have to parse it yourself. The common approach is to accept the raw string (e.g., "1,2,3,4") and use custom logic in your skill implementation to split and transform it into a list before passing it to an API. There aren’t built‑in type conversions, so manual parsing inside your workflow or backend is the recommended way to handle this.

entry.title: Currency Conversion in SAP Datasphere Using Graphical Views
entry.content:
Introduction: Currency Conversion in SAP

In SAP landscapes such as S/4HANA, SAP Datasphere, and SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), handling multiple currencies is a fundamental requirement due to global operations and diverse organizational structures. Business transactions are usually posted in the local currency of the country where the transaction takes place, but reporting and analysis often require additional currency perspectives

SAP systems can support multiple organizational and reporting currencies, depending on system configuration and business requirements, such as:

• Company Code Currency -  The legal reporting currency used for statutory and financial reporting.

• Group (Global) Currency - Used for consolidation and group-level reporting across multiple company codes.

• Profit Center Currency - Enables profitability analysis when a dedicated profit center currency is configured.

• Cost Center Currency - Supports internal cost tracking and management accounting scenarios.

• Transaction Currency - The currency in which the original business transaction is posted.

• Controlling Area Currency - Used for management accounting and controlling across company codes.

• Document Currency - The currency stored at the accounting document level for audit and traceability.

• Functional Currency - Represents the primary currency of business operations, commonly used in management reporting.

To ensure accurate and comparable reporting across entities and regions, these currencies must be converted using well-defined exchange rates.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Currency conversion in SAP Datasphere plays a key role by:

  • Centralizing currency conversion logic
  • Enabling consistent currency handling across models
  • Providing harmonized financial data for analytics and planning in SAC

This makes SAP Datasphere the foundation for reliable multi-currency reporting in modern SAP analytics scenarios.

In SAP Datasphere, currency conversion can be implemented in Graphical Views, Analytical Models, and SQL Views, and can also be applied dynamically at runtime in SAP Analytics Cloud.

In the following post, I will be covering the currency conversion in graphical view with BW as a source system.

Prerequisite:

Based on the source system (BW, S4HANA etc) the ABAP connection should be configured from source to Datasphere system.

Step 1: Import TCUR* tables, views, data flows and remote tables into Datasphere Tenant

Go to Data Builder

Select '+' symbol

Select 'Currency Conversion Views'

Note: In the Source drop down list, based on the type of source different artifacts will be created in Datasphere.

Manual - Local tables and views

SAP S/4 HANA Cloud / On Premise - Local tables, views and data flows will be imported
SAP ABAP connection - Local tables, views, data flows and remote tables will be imported.

Select Connection as ABAP

By selecting ABAP as a source - Local tables, views, data flows and remote tables will be imported.

Click on 'Create' - All the objects will be imported in to Datasphere tenant, localize the objects in a folder to use globally for different spaces.

Note: Naming convention as follows by default, option available to change Business Name

Remote tables - SAP.CURRENCY.RTABLE.TCUR*

Data Flows - SAP.CURRENCY.DATAFLOW.TCUR*

Tables - SAP.CURRENCY.TABLE.TCUR*

Views - SAP.CURRENCY.VIEW.TCUR*

Once you’ve created the currency conversion views and their supporting objects, you can share the TCUR* tables with other spaces. The spaces to which you share these tables will then see your space as an alternative source in the Source field above.

Step 2: Make TCUR* data available from BW to SAP Datasphere

Since the connection type is ABAP with BW, the existing source tables in the BW system must be mapped to their corresponding remote tables in SAP Datasphere.

Select the Remote table which are imported automatically in the system.

Example: For TCURR configuration, the same steps must be repeated for all imported TCUR* tables.

Go to 'Data Builder'

Search 'SAP.CURRENCY.RTABLE.TCURR' table

Open the table and set up the 'Remote' connection details (BW_ABAP : configured ABAP connection from BW to Datasphere)

Note: There are different methods to replicate data into Remote table:

Start Data Replication: Fetches data from the source BW ABAP table (TCURR) into the corresponding remote table in SAP Datasphere and stages it. This step can be included in a task chain. (STAGED)

Enable Real-Time Replication: Reads data virtually from BW into SAP Datasphere on demand, without staging. (VIRTUAL)

Once this step is completed, go to 'Data Builder' -> search for data flow 'SAP.CURRENCY.DATAFLOW.TCURR' -> Select 'Run'

Note: The data flow can be scheduled to run at regular intervals or included in a task chain for daily execution.

Once the data flow is completed (which can be monitored using the icon below), the data can be viewed in the currency view SAP.CURRENCY.VIEW.TCURR, a default SQL-based view provided by SAP

SAP.CURRENCY.VIEW.TCURR (provided by SAP)

 

Step3: Currency conversion in Datasphere views.

Go to 'Data Builder' (select space if applicable)

Select 'New Graphical View' or select if any existing view available which requires currency conversion.

There are three columns:

'Sale_Amount_CompCode' the measure contains amount to be converted

 'Company_Code_Currency' holds the source currency

 'Profit_Center_Currency' holds the target currency

Go to View -> Select the node where currency conversion need to apply -> Add 'fx' step

Select 'fx' step -> Details -> select '+' -> select 'Currency Conversion Column'

Set the Element Properties

Provide Business Name, Technical Name, Data type and Decimal changes

Currency Properties

Source Amount Column - Represents the column which requires conversion in this case 'Sale_Amount_CompCode''

Source Currency - Currency works as a source value  in this case 'Company_Code_Currency'

Target currency - Currency works as a target value in this case 'Profit_Center_Currency'

Reference Date - This is Year/Date to be used as a reference during conversion.

Steps: Different options available and one of the key element

 

Shift

The amount is multiplied to remove decimals. Example 123.45 USD → 12345

Convert

The shifted amount is converted using the exchange rate. Example 12345 × exchange rate

Round

The converted value is rounded based on the target currency’s decimal rules.

Shift Back

The value is divided to restore the correct decimal places.

Note: For any simple currency conversion, recommended to select only 'Convert' and 'Round'

Conversion properties

Client - Client 000 is typically used for currency tables in SAP systems

Conversion Type - Exchange rate type maintained in TCURR (for example, M – Standard rate, P – Budget rate)

Error Handling - Defines the value to be generated if no valid exchange rate is found.

Advanced Properties

Lookup: Regular and Reverse (to be selected if reverse exchange rate is maintained in the system)

Accuracy: Compatibility (Matches classic SAP (ECC/BW) currency results) and Highest (Provides maximum precision for accurate financial reporting)

Date Format: Auto Detect (Automatically picks the correct date format from source data and applies the right exchange rate)

All remaining views relevant to TCUR* will get auto populated by default, as its configured at beginning.

Activate and Deploy the view

Result Output:

Exchange rate from AUD to GBP is maintained as 1.75 in BW system

Data From BW System:

Client

Exchange Type

From

To

Valid From

Exchange Rate

600

P

AUD

GBP

01.01.2025

/1.75

Values are converted using the applicable exchange rate and published in a new column called “Profit Center Amount.”

Conclusion: By centralizing currency conversion logic in SAP Datasphere, organizations can ensure consistent, auditable, and scalable multi-currency reporting across analytical and planning scenarios. This approach simplifies downstream consumption in SAP Analytics Cloud while maintaining alignment with SAP’s standard currency configuration.

 

Thank you for taking the time to read this article. I hope this overview helps you understand and implement currency conversion in SAP Datasphere more effectively. Your feedback, questions, and suggestions are welcome in the comments section.

 

Regards

Lokesh Kumar Pothapola

entry.title: Transitioning to the New China Cash Flow Statement Solution: What You Need to Know
entry.content:
As regulatory reporting requirements continue to evolve, SAP is committed to providing customers with modern, efficient solutions that meet compliance needs while leveraging the latest technology capabilities. For organizations operating in China, an important transition is underway regarding the Cash Flow Statement reporting solution.

Understanding the Evolution

The legacy China Cash Flow Statement solution (IDCNCASH) has served customers well over the years, but technological advancement necessitates modernization. Since S/4HANA CE2111/OP2021, SAP has offered an enhanced solution built on the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) platform, which provides significant improvements across multiple dimensions.

Key Advantages of the New Solution

The DRC-based Cash Flow Statement addresses several limitations of the legacy approach:

  • Performance and Architecture: The new solution leverages SAP HANA's in-memory database capabilities, delivering superior performance even with large data volumes. Unlike the legacy solution, which faced timeout issues with substantial datasets, the modern architecture is designed for scalability.
  • Data Consistency: By sourcing data directly from the ACDOCA table, the new solution ensures alignment with core financial data and other related reports. This eliminates the data inconsistencies that occasionally occurred with the legacy solution's separate data sources.
  • Enhanced Currency Support: While the legacy solution supported only four currency types, the new solution accommodates all currency types assigned to company codes and ledgers in S/4HANA, providing greater flexibility for multinational operations.
  • Proactive Error Detection: The new solution determines offsetting accounts during document posting rather than at report runtime, enabling earlier identification and resolution of issues.
  • Advanced Functionality: Built on the DRC platform, the solution offers scheduled tasks, historical snapshots, analytical views, and customer extension capabilities—features that support more sophisticated reporting workflows.

Migration Timeline and Support

Starting with CE2602, SAP has introduced toggle functionality allowing users to switch between the legacy and new solutions, facilitating a gradual transition.

  • When users open the old version app, a dialog will promote the new version app with functional benefits, and allow a direct switch to the new version app (DRC: Run Statutory Reports).

  • In the new app (DRC), users can submit feedback on their experience and switch back to the old app at any time.

While SAP has extended the transition window to accommodate the large user base, customers are encouraged to plan their migration proactively.

Implementation Resources

SAP has developed comprehensive implementation materials to support the transition, including detailed white papers and video tutorials.

Implementation handbook (white papers) in Chinese/English

Video tutorial: Step-by-Step Implementation Guide: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) Cash Flow Statements

Next Steps

Customers currently using the legacy Cash Flow Statement solution should begin evaluating their migration timeline. Review SAP Note 3700711 - Replacement of Existing 'Cash Flow Statements - China' App with 'China Cash Flow Statements' Report in Document and Reporting Compliance for the latest information on the transition roadmap and consult the implementation handbook for technical guidance.

As with any significant system change, adequate planning and testing are essential. The investment in migration will position your organization to benefit from enhanced performance, improved data consistency, and access to advanced reporting capabilities that support evolving business needs.

For implementation support, please refer to the SAP Help Portal or contact your SAP representative.

 

FAQ:

Q1: What is SAP DRC?

A: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance is a unique and competitive statutory reporting framework that helps you manage your regulatory requirements across the globe. Statutory reporting, a component of SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, is a global solution that enables timely creation, generation, and submission of statutory reports to authorities in the correct format. It ensures compliance with complex and dynamic legal requirements. For more details, refer to SAP Help Portal for Statutory Reporting.

Q2: For S/4HANA Public Cloud customers, does the new DRC Cash Flow Statement require activation of new scope items?

A: Yes.

  • With 1J2, you can generate and download the China Cash Flow Statement in both PDF and TXT(CSV) format.
  • With additional 5XU, you can preview and edit (change the figures and regenerate files with full audit trail), perform analytics, trace back to source documents, and extend the existing report using design time.

Customers need to contact your SAP Account Executive if they have any question related to licenses.

Q3: After the legacy solution is retired, how can customers use the new solution to view historical Cash Flow data?

A: After you activate and configure the DRC cashflow report, you can run the Data Migration Tool (for Public Cloud and OP, or transaction code FIRU_CF_OFF_ACC) to display historical data in the new DRC cashflow report.

Note:

  1. We recommend that you do the solution switch and run the migration program during year-end financial closing or at the beginning of the year.
  2. If you purchased 5XU, you can use the drill-down tool in the analytics view for troubleshooting. If the new solution is configured correctly, the data in the new and legacy solutions should be consistent (legacy solutions use similar offsetting account determination configuration and calculation logic).
  3. This tool only performs data migration. It can't resolve the effort required for customers to implement the new DRC cashflow report.

Q4: What existing legal reports have been replaced by SAP DRC statutory reports so far?

A: SAP Note 2480067 - Replacement of Existing Legal Reports with DRC reports provides information about the legacy ABAP legal reports that statutory reports provided with the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance framework are succeeding.

entry.title: When Synchronous OData Failed for bulk data processing: How I Solved Problem Using OData V4 Async :
entry.content:
The Business Context:

  • BlueYonder sends planning and operational data
  • The data volume is large
  • Data needs to be posted into SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
  • SAP exposes this functionality via OData V2 and V4 APIs

The expectation was simple: “Send data from BlueYonder and create/update records in S/4HANA.” But in reality, it was not that easy.

Introduction:

In real projects, integrations look simple on paper but behave very differently in production.

This blog is about a real-time problem I faced while integrating BlueYonder planning data into SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud using OData V2 Inbound.

The solution was understanding how OData V4 async processing works and using one small but powerful HTTP header.

In this blog, I will explain:

  • What problem I faced
  • Why synchronous processing failed
  • How I implemented async OData V4 processing with API proxy(through URL).
  • What changed after enabling async

 

The Actual Problem

Initially, I implemented the integration in a synchronous way.

What Happened

  • CPI sent data to S/4HANA public cloud using standard OData V2 synchronous Api
  • The backend took time to process the payload
  • CPI waited for the response
  • Requests started failing due to:
    • Timeouts
    • Long-running backend processing
    • Unstable behaviour during peak loads

The Key Issue

OData V2 APIs are synchronous by default. This means:

  • CPI waits until S/4HANA public cloud finishes posting
  • Large payloads increase risk
  • One slow request can block the entire flow This was not acceptable for a production-grade integration.
  • Chunking the payload using General splitter and streaming could increase the number of API Calls and may increase the load on Public cloud.

Design:

While analysing SAP documentation, I came across the asynchronous processing capability of OData V4. SAP provides a standard way to tell the backend: “Accept the request, process it in the background, and do not keep the connection open.”
This is done using the HTTP header: Prefer = respond-async

Step-by-Step Implementation :

Step 1: Receiving HighRadiusData in CPI

  • BlueYonder sends data in Flatten File.
  • CPI receives the data and prepares it for S/4HANA public cloud.

Data structure is aligned to OData V4 expectations At this point, the flow is still normal.

 

 

 

  • *Here I had implemented the Retry mechanism using JMS Queues which is the best practice for the SAP integrations ,If you like you implement it do check out my previous Blog that I had posted in the SAP Community.
    Blog URL – {https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/handling-connectivity-and-recoverable-errors-in-sap-cpi-with-jms-queues/ba-p/14137974}

Step 2: Setting the Async Header (Most Important Step)

In CPI, I used a Content Modifier to add HTTP headers.

Header : Prefer Header Prefer = respond-async This header completely changes how the request is handled.

 

What Happens After Adding respond-async

Once this header is added:

  • CPI sends the request
  • S/4HANA immediately responds with HTTP 202 – Accepted
  • Backend processing continues asynchronously
  • CPI does not wait for the final processing result This single header removed timeout risks completely.

Step 3: Posting Data via API Proxy

* Here, I had used OAuth 2.0 Authentication between Integration suite to API Proxy and between API Proxy and S/4 Hana public cloud I had used Certificate Based Authentication.

 

  • CPI sends the request to an API Proxy
  • The proxy forwards the call to the OData V4 API from SAP Business Accelerator Hub
  • The proxy is created using the URL-based option

Note : OData V4 services do not appear in the API Provider’s service catalog in SAP API Management, so discovery isn’t possible — and that’s why I go with the URL method, so if you want to create proxy for OData V4 public cloud the correct approach is just go with the URL Option don’t fall for this pitfall.

If you want to leverage more on how to create API Proxy with URL option follow SAP Official Documentation.
Official documentation: {https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-api-management/sap-api-management/create-api-proxy-by-providing-direct-target-endpoint-url}

From CPI’s perspective:

  • The request is completed successfully

No long-running connection is maintained

  • What Changed After This Implementation

    Before Async

    • Timeouts during high data volume
    • Unstable integration behaviour
    • Risk of message failures

    After Async

    • Immediate response to CPI
    • Backend processing happens safely
    • No timeout issues
    • Integration became stable and predictable

    This was a real production-grade improvement, not just a theoretical change, I hope this helps you folks that don’t fall for this pitfall in your production.

entry.title: Timeout in RAP Action
entry.content:
I have a Rap app with an action in a table that is partially executed on up to 100,000 items. Processing then takes place in the backend for each location. Since this process takes a certain amount of time per item, it can take several hours for the entire logic to be completed. As a result, the process aborts in the frontend with a timeout.

I solved the problem by running the processing in an RFC module in the background. The customer does not want to extend the session length that causes the timeout. My question now is whether there are other alternatives to solve this problem?

entry.title: Exception aggregation steps and #NOP together (ABAP RAP Analytical Query)
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to define an analytical query in ABAP RAP and I am struggling to have #NOP operation and exception aggregation steps working together in one query.

For simplicity, let's say I have a cube with the following properties: Document, Document Item, Sales and Price. With this simple model, I can define aggregation #SUM for Sales and aggregation #NOP for Price. Let's say I have the following data in my cube:\

Document A, 10, 20, 10
Document A, 20, 40, 10
Document B, 10, 100, 20
Document B, 20, 200, 25

When I ask to get aggregated records by Document and show measures Sales and Price, I get the following two records:

Document A, 60, 10
Document B, 300, <null>

This is all great and what I exactly want to see. The sales get aggregated and Price shows a value only if all items have the same price. Otherwise it does not show the value.

The problem is that my data model is more complex. Let's say each document can be tagged with one or more tags. My cube also has additional attribute Tag. I want provide flexibility to users to have aggregation per tag. Let's say I have the following data in my cube:

Tag One, Document A, 10, 20, 10
Tag One, Document A, 20, 40, 20
Tag Two, Document A, 10, 20, 10
Tag Two, Document A, 20, 40, 20

I have one document, with different prices on each item and the document is associated with two tags. When I ask to get aggregated records by Document and show measures Sales and Price, I get the following record:

Document A, 120, <null>

The aggregation for Price works fine (it shows null as there are different prices for document items). But the aggregation for Sales is double counting as the document has two tags. This is clearly wrong. To fix this problem, I introduced the exception aggregation steps for measure Sales.

@AnalyticsDetails.exceptionAggregationSteps: [ { exceptionAggregationBehavior: #SUM,
exceptionAggregationElements: [ 'Document' ] } ]
Sales

When I ask to get the aggregate records by Document and show measures Sales and Price, I get the following record:

Document A, 60, 10

The aggregation for Sales works again and it does not double count Sales anymore. But the aggregation #NOP stopped working and it shows the value from the first item. I would like to avoid this. The aggregatiobn #NOP is not supported for exceptionAggregationBehavior.

Is there a way how to make #NOP and exception aggregation steps work together? Or is there another way how to model this type of the scenario where the same document can be present multiple times and the summation should prevent any double counting?

The best workaround I could come up with is that I defined two new measures MinPrice and MaxPrice with the corresponding aggregations #MIN and #MAN. Then I defined a new measure with the following formula.

@Aggregation.default: #FORMULA
@EndUserText.label: 'Test'
@AnalyticsDetails.query:{ decimals: 2}
case when $projection.minprice = $projection.maxprice then get_numeric_value(MinPrice) else 0 end as Test,

This almost works as desired. If minimal and maximal prices are the same, it shows the price. Otherwise it shows 0. Here I would prefer null instead of 0. But there are other things that I do not like about this workaround. The hard coded decimals are not nice. And it means that for each measure like Price, I need to define additional 2 measures Min and Max. I have more than 10 fields like this so it quickly gets out of hand. The last concern is about performance of this workaround.

Any suggestions how to achieve this requirement?

Thanks

entry.title: Automating Purchase Requisition Rejection - ME54N and Fiori My Inbox
entry.content:
Background

SAP Note 2012650 – “BAPI_REQUISITION_RELEASE is not possible to reject PR” clearly states that Purchase Requisitions cannot be rejected using BAPI_REQUISITION_RELEASE and that rejection must be performed manually via transaction ME54N.

There are scenarios where manual rejection is not feasible or creates significant operational overhead. In my case, I needed a way to trigger Purchase Requisition rejection programmatically while staying as close as possible to standard behavior.

This requirement led me to search for an alternative approach.

While ME54N is the classic transaction for rejecting PRs, many users also perform approval and rejection activities through Fiori My Inbox. These two entry points do not rely on BAPI_REQUISITION_RELEASE for rejection.

During my analysis, I noticed that the class CL_MM_PUR_PR_APPR_ACTION_BADI plays a central role in handling approval and rejection actions triggered from Fiori. This class became the starting point for my solution.

During my research, I came across a well-written blog describing another approach for Purchase Requisition rejection. Unfortunately, I could not make that solution work in my system, so I explored a different path which resulted in the approach presented here.

Approach

The solution consists of two main steps:

  1. Trigger rejection logic using CL_MM_PUR_PR_APPR_ACTION_BADI

    • This ensures that the approval work item is removed from Fiori My Inbox.

    • The PR is treated consistently with the standard workflow behavior.

  2. Call ME_UPDATE_REQUISITION using classic techniques

    • After the inbox item is cleared, the Purchase Requisition is updated using standard update logic.

    • This mimics the behavior executed when rejection is performed via ME54N.

Important Notes

  • This is not necessarily the best or only approach, but rather one possible alternative when no standard BAPI exists.

  • The provided development is intended as a template only. You can revise and adapt the example class according to your own requirements.
  • It represents a custom automation workaround.

Example Implementation

CLASS zmm_cl_utils_pr DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS : pr_reject
      IMPORTING iv_banfn            TYPE banfn OPTIONAL
      RETURNING VALUE(RV_completed) TYPE abap_bool
      RAISING   cx_mm_pur_requisition.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS get_release_code
      IMPORTING
        iv_banfn              TYPE banfn
      RETURNING
        VALUE(rv_releasecode) TYPE frgab.
    METHODS set_my_inbox_status_rejected
      IMPORTING iv_banfn TYPE banfn
      RAISING   cx_mm_pur_requisition.
    METHODS set_pr_status_rejected
      IMPORTING iv_banfn            TYPE banfn
      RETURNING VALUE(RV_completed) TYPE abap_bool
      RAISING   cx_mm_pur_requisition.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS zmm_cl_utils_pr IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD pr_reject.
    set_my_inbox_status_rejected( iv_banfn = iv_banfn ).
    rv_completed = set_pr_status_rejected( iv_banfn = iv_banfn ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_release_code.
    DATA lv_fname TYPE fieldname.

    SELECT SINGLE frgzu, frgst, frggr
      FROM eban
      WHERE banfn = _banfn
      INTO (ls_eban).
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.
    lv_fname = |T16FS~FRGC{ strlen( ls_eban-frgzu ) + 1 }|.
    SELECT SINGLE (lv_fname) FROM t16fs
      WHERE frggr = _eban-frggr
        AND frgsx = _eban-frgst
      INTO @rv_releasecode.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD set_my_inbox_status_rejected.
    CONSTANTS lc_gname_eban TYPE eqegraname VALUE 'EBAN'.
    DATA lv_wiid          TYPE sww_wiid.
    DATA lv_note          TYPE string.
    DATA lv_save_note     TYPE boolean.
    DATA lv_source_appl   TYPE swc_elem.
    DATA lv_garg          TYPE eqegraarg.
    DATA ls_object        TYPE swr_obj_2.
    DATA lt_enq           TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF seqg3.
    DATA lr_pur_wf_object TYPE REF TO cl_mm_pur_wf_object.

    DATA(lv_like) = |%{ iv_banfn }%|.

    SELECT wi_id, wi_stat FROM swwwihead
      WHERE wi_text LIKE _like
        AND wi_stat    = 'READY'
      ORDER BY wi_cd DESCENDING,
               wi_ct DESCENDING
      INTO TABLE (lt_tab)
      UP TO 1 ROWS.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      RAISE EXCEPTION NEW cx_mm_pur_requisition( textid = VALUE #( msgid = 'SWF_RUN'
                                                                   msgno = '003' ) ).
    ENDIF.

    lv_wiid = lt_tab[ 1 ]-wi_id.
    " -->Get the Purchase Requisition Number, Item from the WorkItem
    CALL FUNCTION 'SAP_WAPI_GET_OBJECTS'
      EXPORTING workitem_id      = lv_wiid
      IMPORTING leading_object_2 = ls_object.
    DATA(lv_pr_num)  = ls_object-instid+0(10).
    DATA(lv_pr_item) = ls_object-instid+10(5).
    IF lv_pr_item IS NOT INITIAL.
      lr_pur_wf_object ?= NEW cl_mm_pur_wf_object_pr_item( iv_object_id = CONV #( ls_object-instid ) ).
    ELSE.
      IF lv_pr_num IS NOT INITIAL.
        lr_pur_wf_object ?= NEW cl_mm_pur_wf_object_pr( iv_object_id = CONV #( ls_object-instid ) ).
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.

    IF lv_pr_item IS NOT INITIAL.
      " Item Level Release
      SELECT SINGLE PurReqnOrigin,CreatedByUser
        FROM I_PurchaseRequisitionItemAPI01
        INTO (ls_origin)
        WHERE PurchaseRequisition     = _pr_num
          AND PurchaseRequisitionItem = _pr_item.
    ELSE.
      " Overall Release
      SELECT PurReqnOrigin,CreatedByUser
        UP TO 1 ROWS
        FROM I_PurchaseRequisitionItemAPI01
        INTO _origin
        WHERE PurchaseRequisition = _pr_num
        " In case if different users have created different items of PR
        " then the Requester would be the First item user.
        ORDER BY PurchaseRequisitionItem.
      ENDSELECT.
    ENDIF.
    " -->Get Decision
    DATA(lv_decision) = CONV swc_elem( if_mm_pur_workflow_c=>gc_decision_0002 ). " REJECTED
    " -->Check if there is a lock on the PR.only in case of overall release.
    IF lv_pr_item IS INITIAL.
      lv_garg = |{ sy-mandt }{ lv_pr_num }*|.
      CALL FUNCTION 'ENQUEUE_READ'
        EXPORTING  gname                 = lc_gname_eban
                   garg                  = lv_garg
                   guname                = ''
        TABLES     enq                   = lt_enq
        EXCEPTIONS communication_failure = 1
                   system_failure        = 2
                   OTHERS                = 3.
      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        IF lt_enq IS NOT INITIAL.
          RAISE EXCEPTION NEW cx_mm_pur_requisition( textid = VALUE #( msgid = 'MMPUR_PR_WFL_COMMON'
                                                                       msgno = '010' ) ).
        ENDIF.
      ELSE.
        RAISE EXCEPTION NEW cx_mm_pur_requisition( textid = VALUE #( msgid = sy-msgid
                                                                     msgno = sy-msgno
                                                                     attr1 = sy-msgv1
                                                                     attr2 = sy-msgv2
                                                                     attr3 = sy-msgv3
                                                                     attr4 = sy-msgv4 ) ).
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.

    IF lr_pur_wf_object IS BOUND.
      lr_pur_wf_object->set_workitem_executed( EXPORTING iv_wiid        = lv_wiid
                                                         iv_decision    = lv_decision
                                                         iv_note        = lv_note
                                                         iv_save_note   = lv_save_note
                                                         iv_source_appl = lv_source_appl
                                               IMPORTING es_return      = DATA(ls_return) ).
      IF ls_return IS NOT INITIAL.
        RAISE EXCEPTION NEW cx_mm_pur_requisition( textid = VALUE #( msgid = ls_return-id
                                                                     msgno = ls_return-number
                                                                     attr1 = ls_return-message_v1
                                                                     attr2 = ls_return-message_v2
                                                                     attr3 = ls_return-message_v3
                                                                     attr4 = ls_return-message_v4 ) ).
      ENDIF.
      DATA(lo_pr_approval) = cl_mm_pur_approval=>get_instance( ).
      lo_pr_approval->gs_exe_src-execution_source = if_mm_pur_workflow_c=>gc_execution_source_inb.
      lo_pr_approval->gs_exe_src-scenario_type    = ls_object-catid.
    ENDIF.
    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'.
    WAIT UP TO '0.1' SECONDS.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD set_pr_status_rejected.
    DATA t_xeban     TYPE mereq_t_ueban.
    DATA s_xeban     TYPE ueban.
    DATA t_xebkn     TYPE mereq_t_uebkn.
    " TODO: variable is assigned but never used (ABAP cleaner)
    DATA t_container TYPE swrtcont.

    SELECT * FROM eban
      INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @t_xeban
      WHERE banfn = _banfn
      ORDER BY PRIMARY KEY.
    CHECK t_xeban IS NOT INITIAL.

    SELECT * FROM ebkn
      INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @t_xebkn
      WHERE banfn = _banfn.

    DATA(t_yeban) = t_xeban.
    DATA(t_yebkn) = t_xebkn.

    READ TABLE t_xeban INTO s_xeban INDEX 1.
    s_xeban-kz    = 'U'.
    s_xeban-banpr = '08'.
    MODIFY t_xeban FROM s_xeban INDEX 1.
    CALL FUNCTION 'ME_UPDATE_REQUISITION'
      TABLES xeban = t_xeban
             xebkn = t_xebkn
             yeban = t_yeban
             yebkn = t_yebkn.

    CALL FUNCTION 'ME_UPDATE_REQUISITION_CHNGDOC'
      TABLES xeban = t_xeban
             xebkn = t_xebkn
             yeban = t_yeban
             yebkn = t_yebkn.

    t_container = VALUE #( LET relcode = get_release_code( iv_banfn ) IN
                            FOR wa IN t_xeban
                            ( element = 'PURCHASINGDOCUMENT' value = wa-bnfpo )
                            ( element = 'PURCHASINGITEM'     value = '00010' )
                            ( element = 'RELEASEGROUP'       value = get_release_code( iv_banfn ) ) ).
    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'.

    DO 100 TIMES.
      WAIT UP TO '0.1' SECONDS.
      SELECT SINGLE  FROM eban
        WHERE banfn = _banfn
          AND banpr = '08' " rejected
        INTO (lv_check).
      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        rv_completed = abap_true.
      ENDIF.
    ENDDO.
    IF lv_check = abap_false.
      RAISE EXCEPTION NEW cx_mm_pur_requisition( textid = VALUE #( msgid = '00'
                                                                   msgno = '001'
                                                                   attr1 = 'Reject could not completed' ) ).
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

Conclusion

Although SAP requires manual rejection of Purchase Requisitions, practical project needs sometimes demand automation.

By leveraging the same building blocks used by Fiori My Inbox and ME54N, it is possible to implement a controlled and consistent workaround for programmatic PR rejection.

This approach does not replace SAP standard behavior, but it provides a pragmatic option when no standard API exists.

entry.title: Build Work Zone Returning 401
entry.content:
I have deployed an app on SAP BTP. The app has a service worker as it is a PWA and served through Build Work Zone Managed App Router. Everything works fine until the authentication cookie expires. Once the cookie expires, the app triggers reloading the index.html page, detects that the user is not logged in anymore and takes us to Build Work Zone login screen. In the authentication process, https://*.ondemand.com/login/callback?authType=ias&code
always gets 401 . If I remove the service worker , it works . Even if the service worker does not intercept any request, just it's presence is breaking the auth flow. 
Is there anyone who can help me figure out what's wrong here. Thanks for your time and suggestion.

Could you please confirm if there is a supported SAP standard exit, Business Add-In, or enhancement spot available to facilitate this requirement without modification to the standard object?

entry.title: Setting Up Document Grounding with Cloud Integration Automation service: Making Joule context-aware
entry.content:
While Document Grounding has been used to make Joule context-aware with specific business data, the latest development is that integrating Document Grounding with Joule is now fully automated by Cloud Integration Automation service.

In this blog you will find how Cloud Integration Automation service completely automates the integration of Document Grounding with Joule and SharePoint as the data source, hence making Joule context aware.

For more information regarding Document Grounding refer the blogpost : Joule – Getting Started with Document Grounding

Setting up Document Grounding using Cloud Integration Automation service

To set up Document Grounding, you would need to configure many steps as explained in Blog, however, lets now discover how Cloud Integration Automation service can simplify the whole process.

Prerequisites:

  1. Joule instance should be available in one of the SAP BTP subaccounts
  2. You have prepared SharePoint as mentioned in this Blog step “6. Prepare SharePoint Integration “.

Note: You will use SharePoint details later in the Cloud Integration Automation service workflow

Step 1: Subscribe Cloud Integration Automation service via BTP service marketplace. More details can be found : What is Cloud Integration Automation Service? | SAP Help Portal.

Step 2: Once subscribed, launch Cloud Integration Automation service application and click on “Plan for Integration”

Image 1

Step 3: Search for “Document Grounding” scenario

Image 2

You can view scenario related details such as “Description”, “Scenario Entities”, “Scenario Tasks”.

Image 3

Step 4: Click on “Select Systems”.

Image 4

Step 5: Provide system details and “Generate Workflow”

  1. Provide SAP BTP system details

Image 5

    2.  Click on Generate workflow ->Background Execution

Image 6

Note: In the above step, Cloud Integration Automation service will generate a fully automated workflow, and users just need to provide few information to complete the integration.

Click on Next step

Select SharePoint as the data source as highlighted in the below screen shot.

Image 8

Click on Next Step

Image 9

Note: In this step Cloud Integration Automation service will create Share point destination, hence you will use share point information such as Share Point client ID, Share Point Client Secrete, SharePoint Tenant ID, Site URL from the Share Point application that you are using as the data source. You can fetch SharePoint details by referring to the screen shots.

Client ID:

Image 10

Client Secrete:

Image 11

Tenant ID:

Image 12

SharePoint URL:

Image 13

Once you provide all the task parameters click on Next step

Image 14

Click on Confirm

Image 15

Image 16

Step 7: Navigate to Scenario Monitoring to check the workflow status.

Image 17

Here you can observe that all the tasks that are needed for this integration are listed and implemented sequentially.

 Task list:

Task Name

Task Status


Assign Entitlement for Document Grounding Service

Automated

Create Grounding Service Instance with a Service Key

Automated

Create Cloud Identity Service Instance with a Service Key

Automated

Create Destination Service Instance with a Service Key

Automated

Create Destination in BTP for Existing SharePoint

Automated

Create Destination in BTP for Existing SAP Document Management Service

Automated

Create Pipeline for SharePoint

Automated

Create Pipeline for SAP Document Management Service

Automated

 

Upon implementation of all the tasks, Document Grounding is fully integrated with Joule and is context aware with SharePoint as the data source.

Step 8: Go to BTP subaccount to check the following:

1. Instances (groundigcli, grounding Des, identityService) are created along with their service keys

2. Document Grounding destination (docu-grounding-dest-sh) is created

Image 19

Step 9: Launch Joule and ask Joule questions.  You can now get context-aware answers and with links to its document sources.

Image 20

 

Useful Links

 What is Cloud Integration Automation Service? | SAP Help Portal

Joule provisioning with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private using Cloud Integration Automation service
